water_temp_read.read: Water is warming; trout may favor more oxygenated water.

light_pressure_read.read: Light and pressure do not strongly push the outlook either way.

stocking_read.read: Recent stocking supports eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs.

angler_analysis.expected_trout_behavior: Expect trout to favor the most comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover when light is high.
angler_analysis.presentation_style: Prioritize clean drifts, smaller natural patterns, and stocked-trout attractors where appropriate.

fly_reasoning.egg_pattern: Recent stocking makes eggs a logical confidence fly.
fly_reasoning.squirmy_worm: A simple stocked-trout attractor and good dirty-water option.
fly_reasoning.duracell: A strong attractor nymph when fish will move a little but are not fully aggressive.

generated_text.quick_read: The Chattahoochee River offers fair to good trout fishing with warming water favoring oxygenated spots. Recent stocking increases success using egg patterns and stocked trout attractors.
generated_text.best_window: Day 2 morning is best due to moderate sky cover, low wind, and low rain probability enhancing trout activity in riffles and oxygenated runs.
generated_text.current_conditions: Water temperature is 62.6°F, flow is 899 cfs, gage height is 2.03 ft, and turbidity is 5.8 NTU.
generated_text.weather_setup: Mostly clear skies with 19.62% average cover, pressure steady at 30.04 inHg with neutral trend, west wind averaging 5.6 mph, and low rain chance of 27% with minimal expected accumulation.
generated_text.what_this_means: Trout will favor riffles, pocket water, and oxygenated runs while staying near structure and seams. Use early and late fishing windows with active and balanced nymph presentations. Prioritize clean drifts and smaller naturals like egg patterns, squirmy worms, and duracell attractors to match recent stocking. No specific caution notes apply.
